name: St. Ann'S Arlington
description_short: Saint Ann's Arlington Federal Credit Union is a small, volunteer-staffed NCUA-insured credit union serving the Saint Ann community since 1949, offering savings accounts, auto loans, and unsecured personal loans.
description_long:
Saint Ann's Arlington Federal Credit Union (SAAFCU) was established in 1949 and has served the Saint Ann Catholic community for over 75 years. As a not-for-profit, member-owned cooperative, it operates under federal credit union charter with NCUA insurance protection. The organization is unique in being mostly volunteer-staffed by parishioners, guided by a nine-member volunteer Board of Directors, with only a paid manager (Mary Green) handling day-to-day operations. The credit union currently has approximately $3.5 million in assets and 450 members.

SAAFCU offers share savings accounts that earn quarterly dividends (interest), federally insured by the NCUA. On the lending side, they provide new and used auto loans (up to 100% of purchase price), auto title loans (up to 90% of vehicle valuation), and unsecured signature loans (up to $10,000 individually or $20,000 for joint accounts). All loans feature competitive rates and a simple, no-fee application process. Members can submit applications via email or in-person at the teller window during Sunday hours.

What distinguishes SAAFCU is its hyper-local focus and community integration. Membership is exclusively open to Saint Ann Catholic Church parishioners, parents of Saint Ann Catholic School students, employees of both organizations, and their immediate families. This narrow membership criterion creates a tight-knit financial community with shared values. The credit union also provides free financial counseling and education to members, and maintains transparent governance with regular annual meetings and published bylaws available to members.

SAAFCU is best suited for members of the Saint Ann Catholic community seeking simple, straightforward financial services without fees or complexity. However, prospective members outside the Catholic community cannot join, and as a small credit union, product offerings are limited compared to larger financial institutions. The volunteer model ensures mission-driven service but may limit operational hours and availability of advanced digital banking features.
services: Member share savings accounts with quarterly dividend payments
New auto loans (up to 100% of purchase price)
Auto title loans (up to 90% of vehicle valuation)
Unsecured personal signature loans (up to $10,000 individual / $20,000 joint)
In-person loan application submission at teller window
Annual membership meetings with Board elections and reports
pros: NCUA-insured member share accounts with quarterly dividend payments
Auto loans available up to 100% of vehicle purchase price with competitive rates
No-fee loan application process via email or in-person submission
Free financial counseling and education services for all members
Nearly 75-year track record serving the Saint Ann community since 1949
Volunteer-led Board of Directors ensures member-focused governance and transparency
Small, tight-knit community focus reduces bureaucracy and enables personalized service
cons: Membership is strictly limited to Saint Ann Catholic Church parishioners, school families, and employees—community outsiders cannot join
Only $3.5 million in assets and 450 members limits lending capacity and product breadth compared to larger credit unions
Limited documented hours; loan applications only accepted during Sunday teller windows or via email, suggesting restricted accessibility
As a mostly volunteer-staffed organization, operational capacity and response times may be slower than professional credit unions
No mention of digital banking, mobile apps, or online account management features on their website
best_for: Saint Ann Catholic Church parishioners seeking simple savings accounts and auto financing with community oversight
Parents of Saint Ann Catholic School students needing personal loans or auto loans within a faith-based financial cooperative
Employees of Saint Ann Catholic Church or School wanting to access member-owned credit union services with transparent governance
Members of the extended Catholic community seeking alternatives to traditional banks with emphasis on shared values and mission
diagnosis: Saint Ann's Arlington Federal Credit Union is ideal for members of the Saint Ann Catholic community (parishioners, school families, and employees) seeking straightforward savings accounts, auto financing, and personal loans within a member-owned, mission-driven cooperative. The critical caveat is that membership is exclusively restricted to the Catholic community; outside applicants cannot join regardless of creditworthiness or interest.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
